environment_note: Local dev stack could not be brought up in this sandbox (/run/duraclaw/sessions is read-only). Browser-interactive VPs deferred to manual sign-off; static + test-suite VPs executed here.

VP3_field_count.description: Adding hypothetical runner_uptime_ms field requires exactly 2 files (collection + consumer).
VP3_field_count.reason: hypothetical probe; spec-review-level verification (B-CLIENT field-count is a design invariant, not a runnable check)

VP4_dead_code.description: No dead references to session-status-collection or sessions-collection shim.

VP9_build_typecheck_test.description: pnpm typecheck + build + test all pass.

VP1_opfs_persists_reload.description: Session status bar hydrates from OPFS before WS reconnects.
VP1_opfs_persists_reload.reason: requires chrome-devtools-axi + live dev stack; sandbox blocks dev-up.sh

VP2_tab_switch_no_flash.description: Tab switches show populated status bar in first snapshot.

VP5_sidebar_live_status.description: Sidebar card shows live running/idle status within 2s.

VP6_notification_watcher.description: Notification bell shows unread count on running→idle / any→waiting_gate.

VP7_display_state_consistency.description: StatusBar, sidebar, and tab bar show consistent status indicators.
VP7_display_state_consistency.reason: All three surfaces now call deriveDisplayState(state, wsReadyState) — consistency is a compile-time invariant. Unit test display-state.test.ts covers all 11 variants. Visual parity still wants a human eye on the rendered UI.

code_review.non_blocker: use-coding-agent.ts:379 returns a derived wsReadyState = state ? 1 : 0 in its hook-return shape; no production consumer reads it (only test mocks). Left as-is to avoid scope creep.

overall_verdict: READY FOR MERGE — all static + test-level VPs pass; browser-interactive VPs (VP1/VP2/VP5/VP6/VP7) need manual sign-off on a live dev stack before final merge.
